methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pyka is a Series B-stage autonomous drone company founded by Michael Norcia, Chuma Ogunwole, Kyle Moore, and Nathan White. The company has raised $40M to date. The relevant peer universe in Drones & Autonomous comprises 7 private companies, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Manna Aero. No public comparables exist in this subsector.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($40M) as a proxy for post-money valuation at Series A, we apply a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $76M. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while four same-sector peers exist, the narrow filter criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) returned fewer than three datapoints, making sectoral median comparables unreliable and potentially misleading.

This analysis supports a valuation range of $65M (low) to $87M (high), with a median point estimate of $76M. The single-method reliance introduces material model risk. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for autonomous vehicle subsector (which may differ from the baseline 1.9x) and the absence of sufficient same-stage peer data to validate relative positioning. Refinement would require either disclosed valuations from comparable Series B rounds in autonomous systems or earlier-stage transaction data to calibrate the progression multiple.
